 {"id":"bd-dkff","title":"demo_showcase page: Dashboard (metrics, status, animations)","description":"The flagship “wow” page: a premium monitoring-style dashboard that immediately communicates polish, usefulness, and technical depth.

Why:
- First impressions matter: on first launch, the dashboard should make the project look inevitable.
- A dashboard is the best place to integrate *everything at once*: layout, styling, animation, real-time updates, and drill-down.

What this page must showcase (integrated):
- lipgloss: dense but readable card/grid layout, borders, alignment, width-aware wrapping/truncation, theme slots.
- bubbles: progress/spinner widgets embedded in cards, a compact table/list for “top incidents / hot services”, viewport for detail text.
- harmonica: smooth animated counters/progress (deltas, easing), attention cues (subtle pulse on state changes).
- bubbletea: timed updates (tick), resize responsiveness, optional mouse interactions.
- charmed_log/toasts: state transitions emit a toast/alert and optionally log an event.

Core UX requirements:
- Multiple “cards” with distinct semantics (e.g., Requests, Latency, Error Rate, Job Throughput, Incidents).
- Visible health states (ok/warn/error) with consistent color + iconography.
- Drill-down: selecting/clicking a card opens a modal/panel with detail + explanation.
- Responsive: reflow to 1-column / 2-column / 3-column depending on terminal width.

Data requirements:
- Driven by the deterministic simulation engine (seeded) so demos/tests are reproducible.
- Includes plausible trends (up/down arrows), thresholds, and at least one periodic “incident”.

Acceptance:
- Looks and feels like a production ops dashboard.
- Values visibly change over time without flicker or input lag.
- State transitions are obvious (animated + toast), but never noisy.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - health-state derivation (thresholds + hysteresis if used)
  - trend calculation (delta over N ticks)
  - responsive layout breakpoints (given width/height)
- E2E:
  - covered by smoke tour
  - additional assertions in the settings/theme scenario should confirm dashboard updates immediately after theme/toggle changes.

Notes:
- Prefer “information clarity” motion (animate toward truth) over gratuitous animation.
- Keep all motion disable-able via `--no-animations` / reduce motion.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:15.448098103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:33:30.045415432Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:33:30.045346414Z","close_reason":"Verified complete: dashboard.rs (1,846 LOC) implements premium ops dashboard with: multiple stat cards, services/deployments/jobs sections, health states (Healthy/Degraded/Unhealthy), Animator integration for smooth transitions, responsive width-based layout, drill-down via DetailsPanel, simulation-driven deterministic data. Tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dkff","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-eo3i","title":"demo_showcase: asset strategy (docs, fixtures, icons)","description":"Decide where demo content lives and how it is loaded.

Constraints:
- Keep the repo tidy: no random extra files at root.
- Avoid runtime network dependencies.
- E2E tests must be deterministic (no dependency on the developer’s home directory).

We need assets for:
- Docs page markdown (multi-section, includes code blocks, tables, links).
- Wizard copy (prompts, tooltips, validation errors).
- Files page fixtures (small deterministic directory tree with:
  - a sample config file
  - a sample log file
  - a sample markdown file for preview
  - a couple nested folders + hidden file edge cases)
- Optional: small icon glyph reference / ASCII art.

Options:
- Embed via `include_str!` / `include_bytes!` (simple, zero runtime I/O).
- Or keep `crates/demo_showcase/assets/` and load at runtime (more editable, but requires I/O and paths).
- Hybrid: embed for runtime, but keep sources in assets for readability.

Acceptance:
- We can ship rich docs/content without external dependencies.
- E2E scenarios (especially Files) can rely on deterministic fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:31.900402267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:48:37.276212852Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:48:37.276140708Z","close_reason":"Implemented asset strategy: assets.rs module with compile-time embedding via include_str!, VirtualEntry tree for FilePicker demo, docs for glamour, fixtures for file browser. Created assets/ directory with docs, config, logs, and nested fixtures.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["assets","build","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-eo3i","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-eo3i","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-fdf3","title":"Files: integrate bubbles FilePicker","description":"Implement file browsing using `bubbles::filepicker`.

Requirements:
- Start directory selection:
  - Default to a deterministic demo fixture directory (so screenshots/E2E are stable).
  - Allow overriding via Config to browse a real path.
- Respect hidden files toggle.

Notes:
- Tie this to the project’s asset strategy: the Files page should have a small built-in fixture tree to showcase preview/rendering and to support CI tests.

Acceptance:
- Keyboard navigation works; selection updates preview.
- In headless/E2E mode, the same file list is available every run.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:17.806118901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:00:37.807916040Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:00:37.807845619Z","close_reason":"FilePicker is fully integrated in pages/files.rs with virtual fixture tree for E2E stability, navigation (j/k/l/h), hidden files toggle, and preview pane.  {"id":"bd-gbps","title":"Unit tests: Config parsing + env/CLI precedence","description":"Add focused unit tests for demo_showcase runtime configuration.

Why:
- The demo will have many toggles; config bugs create confusing UX and flaky tests.
- We want deterministic, documented precedence rules.

Test coverage (minimum):
- Defaults: Config created with no args/env is sane.
- CLI parsing: each flag maps correctly into Config.
- Precedence rules:
  - CLI overrides env where sensible.
  - `NO_COLOR` forces no-color unless explicitly overridden (if we support `--force-color`).
  - `--theme-file` must override `--theme` preset.
- Validation: invalid values (bad seed, bad theme name) produce clean errors.

Acceptance:
- Tests do not require a TTY.
- Failures include actionable assertions/messages.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:12.286603748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:13.569277094Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:13.569146019Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gbps","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-gbps","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
